content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

July 10, 1985

Michael A. Oliver
Acting City Clerk
Civic Center
835 East 14th Street
San Leandro, CA 94577

Re: Our File No. A-85-137

Dear Mr. Oliver:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of the
advice I provided to you on June 5, 1985, regarding the filing of
"threshold" campaign statements by candidates and officeholders
pursuant to the Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections
81000-91015).

Specifically, you asked whether the reference in Part II.B.
of the 1985 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions
to contributions or independent expenditures of $100 or more made
to or on behalf of a controlled committee refers to the candidate's
own controlled committee or to controlled committees of other
candidates.

Government Code Section 84200(b), which specifies when
"threshold" statements must be filed, provides that during any
six-month period in which a candidate or officeholder is not
being voted upon, threshold statements are required only if the
candidate or officeholder receives or spends $500 or more, and
also makes a contribution or expenditure of $100 or more to sup-
port or oppose another candidate, officeholder or a ballot measure.
Therefore, if all expenditures made during the six-month period
by the candidate or officeholder and his or her controlled committee
are in connection with the candidate's or officeholder's own committee
campaign activities, threshold statements are not required; only
a semi-annual statement will be filed for that six-month period.

Enclosed are the 1985 Information Manuals you requested.
Please contact me if you have further questions.

Sincerely,

Carla Wardlow
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures

City of San Leandro
Civic Center, 835 E. 14th Street
San Leandro, California 94577

Office of City Manager     415-577-3351


June 6, 1985


Ms. Carla Wardlow
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Wardlow:

     I appreciate your assistance on the telephone the other day, regard-
ing the question on under what circumstances officeholders and candidates
running for office in 1985 are required to file "Threshold Statements"
under current legislation.

     I would greatly appreciate your statement regarding the applicability
of current Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) regulations regarding the following. The subject is interpretation of Section B of the 1985
FPPC Information Manual (page 7). This section (B) defines the filing
schedules for officeholders, candidates, and ballot measures not being
voted upon in 1985, their controlled committees, and committees primarily
formed to support or oppose such candidates or measures. The sub-section
on "Semi-Annual Statements" is straightforward, as are the filing
deadlines.

     The section on "Threshold Statements" is the focus of the current
question. The section defining the "Method for Calculating Threshold"
states:

     "All officeholders, candidates, controlled committees,
     and committees primarily formed to support or oppose an
     officeholder, candidate or ballot measure covered by
     this section must file a threshold statement whenever
     they make expenditures of $500 or more or receive or
     make contributions of $500 or more, AND they make a
     contribution of $100 or more to, or make an independent
     expenditure of $100 or more on behalf of, at least one
     other candidate, controlled committee or committee
     primarily formed to support or oppose a candidate or a
     ballot measure during the threshold period for such
     statement."

-2-

The question is whether the reference to "...controlled committee" or
"committee primarily formed..." in the section following "AND" refers to
the candidate's own controlled committee or whether it refers to the con-
trolled committees of other candidates.

In addition to your response to this question, please provide ten
1985 information Manuals prepared by the Fair Political Practices Commis-
sion so I can distribute them to the City Council members.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Michael A. Oliver
Senior Administrative
Assistant and Acting
City Clerk

MAO:mt

cc: City Council
    City Manager
    City Attorney

sections.question: Specifically, you asked whether the reference in Part II.B. of the 1985 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions to contributions or independent expenditures of $100 or more made to or on behalf of a controlled committee refers to the candidate's own controlled committee or to controlled committees of other candidates.
sections.conclusion: Therefore, if all expenditures made during the six-month period by the candidate or officeholder and his or her controlled committee are in connection with the candidate's or officeholder's own campaign activities, threshold statements are not required; only a semi-annual statement will be filed for that six-month period.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the $100 or more threshold for contributions or independent expenditures to a controlled committee refers to the candidate's own committee or to committees of other candidates.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Threshold campaign statements are not required if all expenditures are in connection with the candidate's own campaign activities and no contribution or expenditure of $100 or more is made to support or oppose another candidate, officeholder, or ballot measure.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION headers present; sections extracted from body text. Letter is dated July 10, 1985 and confirms prior verbal advice from June 5, 1985. File number A-85-137.

QUESTION: Specifically, you asked whether the reference in Part II.B. of the 1985 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions to contributions or independent expenditures of $100 or more made to or on behalf of a controlled committee refers to the candidate's own controlled committee or to controlled committees of other candidates.

CONCLUSION: Therefore, if all expenditures made during the six-month period by the candidate or officeholder and his or her controlled committee are in connection with the candidate's or officeholder's own campaign activities, threshold statements are not required; only a semi-annual statement will be filed for that six-month period.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms advice regarding when threshold campaign statements must be filed under the Political Reform Act, specifically clarifying that the $100 or more threshold for contributions to controlled committees applies to contributions to other candidates' committees, not the candidate's own.
